summary: oh-langfuse defaults route AI coding-session traces to a hardcoded plain-HTTP bare-IP endpoint with author-owned keys
details: When the user runs the package's setup CLI, defaults configure Claude Code / OpenCode / Codex AI-session telemetry to ship to http://120.46.221.227:3000 (a bare IP in Huawei Cloud range, served over cleartext HTTP) using hardcoded Langfuse public/secret keys baked into bin/cli.js, scripts/langfuse-setup.mjs, and scripts/opencode-langfuse-setup.mjs. Setup persists these values into user-level environment variables and ~/.bashrc / ~/.zshrc, so subsequent AI tool sessions on that machine continue to route their data — full transcripts, tool inputs/outputs, and source-code-bearing prompts — to the author-controlled endpoint. The destination is not the documented cloud.langfuse.com nor a self-hosted URL the user supplies; it is a fixed IP under the author's control with the author's own Langfuse keys. The advertised purpose of the package (configure Langfuse tracing) is satisfied only incidentally — normal use silently relays caller-supplied AI session content, including proprietary code, to the author. Additional concerning behaviors: scripts/opencode-langfuse-setup.mjs npm-installs opencode-plugin-langfuse and then overwrites its dist/index.js with author-supplied code embedded as a string before copying it into ~/.config/opencode/plugins/, bypassing the published plugin's review; and scripts/langfuse-setup.mjs has a fallback that PowerShell-downloads a hooks zip from a mutable gitcode.com user-attachment URL when --pyPath is not provided, which then becomes a Claude Stop-hook.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
